name.es: Melonpan (bollo dulce con corteza de galleta)
name.fr: Melonpan (petit pain sucré à croûte de biscuit)

description.en: A Japanese sweet bun named for its cross-hatched, cantaloupe-like crust rather than any melon flavor. A soft milk-bread center is wrapped in a separate cookie dough that bakes into a crisp, sugary shell, giving one bun two textures in every bite.
description.de: Ein japanisches süßes Brötchen, benannt nach seiner rautenförmig geritzten, melonenartigen Kruste, nicht nach Melonengeschmack. Ein weicher Milchbrot-Kern steckt in einem separaten Keksteig, der knusprig und zuckrig bäckt: zwei Texturen in jedem Bissen.
description.es: Un bollo dulce japonés llamado así por su corteza cuadriculada que recuerda a un melón, no por su sabor. Un centro suave de pan de leche se envuelve en masa de galleta aparte, horneada hasta quedar crujiente y azucarada: dos texturas en cada bocado.
description.fr: Un petit pain sucré japonais nommé d'après sa croûte quadrillée évoquant un melon, sans aucun goût de melon. Un cœur moelleux de pain au lait est enveloppé d'une pâte à biscuit séparée qui cuit en une coque croustillante et sucrée, offrant deux textures à chaque bouchée.

origin.note: Part of the kashipan (sweet bread) family sold at every Japanese bakery and convenience store; regional cousins swap the cookie crust for cocoa or matcha versions.

ingredients.note: 225g bread flour + 25g cake flour, for the bread dough

label.es: harina floja (para la corteza de galleta)

ingredients.note: for rolling the shaped buns before scoring

yield.shape: round bun, cross-hatched cookie crust, ~9 cm

keeping.note: The cookie crust is crispest the day it is baked and softens as it sits; still freezes and reheats acceptably in a toaster oven.

method.title: Mix bread dough
method.summary: Combine bread flour, cake flour, sugar, salt and yeast; stir in egg, milk and water, then knead in the butter until a smooth, elastic dough forms.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Cover and proof about 1-2 hours until doubled.

method.title: Make the cookie dough
method.summary: Cream butter and sugar, beat in the egg, then fold in sifted cake flour and baking powder to a soft, non-sticky dough.

method.title: Divide and rest
method.summary: Divide the bread dough into 10 pieces, shape into balls, and rest 15 minutes; divide the cookie dough into 10 matching pieces.

method.title: Wrap and score
method.summary: Flatten a piece of cookie dough into a disc, wrap it over a bread ball, roll the bun in sugar, then score a crosshatch pattern on top.

method.title: Final proof
method.summary: Proof about 50 minutes until the buns puff to one and a half times their size.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 13-15 minutes until the cookie crust turns light golden.

source.adaptation: Formula percentages describe the yeasted bread dough only; the cookie-dough crust is listed separately and excluded from the formula, since it isn't a bread dough. Salt estimated at standard density.
